nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich areas of lagoon and seaward reefs, generally to about 15 m depth

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting frozen, freeze-dried, fresh, and flake foods in captivity. A varied diet including zooplankton, small crustaceans such as mysis and artemia, and regular vegetable-based foods is generally advised. Vitamin supplements are recommended. Newly added individuals may initially refuse food and tend to benefit from live foods or frequent small feedings to encourage acceptance.

avoidWith: other Bannerfish species unless introduced simultaneously

summary: A striking Indian Ocean butterflyfish with bold black, white, and yellow banding and distinctive forehead horns. Generally considered peaceful and is commonly kept in FOWLR setups; reef compatibility is uncertain due to reports of coral and tubeworm nipping.
careNotes:
The Phantom Bannerfish is generally considered shy when first introduced and tends to benefit from plenty of hiding places in the rockwork. Over time, individuals typically become more bold and comfortable in the aquarium. Can be kept singly, in pairs, or in small groups in larger tanks, though territorial behavior has been reported when housed with other Bannerfish species; simultaneous introduction is generally advised to reduce aggression. This species tends to eat tubeworms and has been reported to nibble at clams (including Tridacna spp.), so reef tank compatibility should be considered carefully.

Temperature note: Hobby sources (LiveAquaria, SaltwaterFish.com) consistently report a care range of 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), while FishBase records wild habitat temperatures of 28.1–29.4°C. These figures represent different things — the wild ocean surface temperature in the Indian Ocean versus the commonly advised aquarium range. The values used here (24–27°C) represent an average across all sources, but this spread is notable enough for reviewer attention.

Tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 125 gallons (~473 L), SaltwaterFish.com recommends 120 gallons (~454 L), and ReefApp recommends a 400 L minimum. Given the species' active swimming nature and need for open space, 500 L is used here as a cautious rounded figure.

Adult size: FishBase and ReefApp list the maximum at 17 cm total length; SaltwaterFish.com states 7–10 inches in captivity (approximately 18–25 cm). The range used here (12–18 cm) reflects the commonly traded size range, biased toward the FishBase-confirmed maximum.

The species is oviparous and pairs during breeding season according to FishBase. Breeding in home aquariums has not been well documented.
breedingNotes: Reported to be oviparous in the wild, forming pairs during the breeding season. Captive breeding has not been well documented and is generally considered very difficult to achieve in home aquariums.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (confirmed 17 cm max), native range, taxonomy, and wild habitat temperature. Wild temperature of 28.1–29.4°C noted; significantly warmer than hobby care recommendations.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), tank size (120 gal), and lifespan (5–7 years). Values consistent with LiveAquaria.

additional.name: ReefApp
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (17 cm), tank size (minimum 400 L), native range (East/West Indian Ocean, Australia, Red Sea, Indonesia), and dietary preferences. Tank minimum of 400 L used in averaging.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; forms pairs during breeding season in the wild. Captive breeding has not been documented in home aquariums.
